text: The attempted murder of Malala Yousafzai on October 9, 2012, was carried out by members of the Tehrik-i-Taliban Pakistan (TTP), specifically by a gunman named Ataullah Khan. The attack was ordered by the TTP leadership because of Malala’s outspoken advocacy for girls’ education and her criticism of the Taliban’s policies in Pakistan’s Swat Valley. The TTP publicly claimed responsibility for the attack shortly after it happened. There is no credible evidence suggesting anyone other than the TTP was behind the attempt on her life.
